startPosition: standing tall, weight on the balls of the feet, ankles dorsiflexed, arms bent ~90° at the elbows ready to drive in a running rhythm
endPosition: one knee punched up to thigh-horizontal (~90° hip flexion) with the stance foot stamping the ground; in B-skip the raised leg then actively extends at the knee and sweeps back into hip extension (hamstring 'paw-back') to strike the ground under the hip
rom: cyclical hip flexion 0→~90° with rapid ankle plantarflexion push-off; B-skip adds terminal knee extension then active hip extension paw-back through the stance

startPosition: standing tall with a long trunk, foot dorsiflexed, arms bent ~90° at the elbows in running posture
endPosition: lead knee driven up to thigh-horizontal (~90° hip flexion) while the stance foot makes brief, sharp ground contact and pops off
rom: cyclical hip flexion 0→~90° paired with plantarflexor push-off; short ground-contact skipping cadence

startPosition: seated (or standing) with the foot flat on the floor, all five toes resting down
endPosition: big toe (hallux) lifted/extended to end range while the lesser four toes stay pressed firmly into the floor, held 3 s at the top
rom: isolated first-MTP extension from 0° to comfortable end range (~50–70°); lesser toes held at 0° (no motion)

startPosition: supine, both legs extended flat on the floor, arms at the sides, pelvis level and both PSIS pressed down equally
endPosition: one leg raised straight to ~60° hip flexion with the knee held in full extension, while the opposite leg stays pinned flat and the pelvis stays level (no rotation)
rom: hip flexion 0→~60° of the moving leg with the knee locked in extension; contralateral hip held at neutral

startPosition: standing on a step edge with the forefoot on the edge and the affected ankle raised into plantarflexion (heel up), knee locked straight for gastrocnemius bias or bent for soleus bias, free hand resting on a rail
endPosition: heel lowered slowly over ~3 s to well below the level of the step into full ankle dorsiflexion
rom: eccentric ankle excursion from top-of-plantarflexion through past-neutral dorsiflexion below the step edge (~full available dorsiflexion)

startPosition: seated upright with a tall, quiet spine, one hand raised so the thumb and ring finger can occlude alternate nostrils
endPosition: same sustained tall seated posture maintained through the slow inhale-left / exhale-right / inhale-right / exhale-left cycle
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; motion limited to diaphragmatic and rib-cage respiratory expansion

startPosition: seated with the leg extended and the shin quiet, resistance band around the forefoot (anchored forward for dorsiflexion, around the sole for plantarflexion), ankle at neutral
endPosition: dorsiflexion — toes pulled up toward the shin to ~20°; plantarflexion — ball of the foot pressed down like a gas pedal to ~40–50°, against band resistance
rom: isolated ankle dorsiflexion 0→~20° and plantarflexion 0→~45°, shin held still

startPosition: seated with the leg extended and the shin stabilized, resistance band anchored laterally for eversion or medially for inversion, foot in neutral
endPosition: eversion — sole swept outward (little-toe side up) to end range; inversion — sole swept inward (big-toe side up) to end range, against the band
rom: subtalar/ankle eversion 0→~15–20° and inversion 0→~20–30°, isolated at the ankle

startPosition: seated with resistance band around the foot and the shin held quiet, foot in neutral
endPosition: sole turned inward to end-range inversion, then rolled outward to end-range eversion against band resistance
rom: ankle inversion 0→~20–30° and eversion 0→~15–20°, motion isolated to the foot

startPosition: standing with feet hip-width apart, weight balanced evenly through both feet
endPosition: weight rocked slowly and slightly forward onto the balls of the feet, then back onto the heels, attending to proprioceptive/interoceptive feedback (eyes open or closed)
rom: small-amplitude ankle dorsiflexion↔plantarflexion weight shift at slow tempo; minimal excursion, attention-focused

startPosition: seated (or standing) with the foot flat on the floor and the toes long and relaxed
endPosition: ball of the foot drawn toward the heel so the medial longitudinal arch lifts and the foot 'shortens', toes kept uncurled, held 5 s
rom: intrinsic 'short-foot' arch elevation with minimal joint excursion; no toe flexion/extension

startPosition: standing with the barbell racked across the upper traps (high-bar) or scapular spine (low-bar), feet shoulder-width, toes turned slightly out, trunk braced, bar over midfoot
endPosition: bottom position with hips and knees flexed together until the hip crease is at or below the top of the knee, spine neutral and bar still over midfoot, then driven back to full standing extension
rom: hip flexion to ≥90° (crease at/below knee) and knee flexion to ~parallel or deeper, returning to full hip and knee extension

startPosition: standing tall with a loaded backpack cinched snug high on both shoulders, load hugging the spine, ribs stacked over pelvis, trunk lightly braced
endPosition: same upright, tall-spine carrying posture maintained while walking at a steady controlled pace for the set distance/time, resisting forward trunk collapse as fatigue builds
rom: isometric trunk/postural stabilization with minimal spinal excursion, superimposed on cyclical hip/knee/ankle gait range

startPosition: standing tall at one end of a clear walkway, feet hip-width apart, knees softly unlocked, trunk upright, weight balanced over both feet
endPosition: having stepped in reverse along the line, trailing toe reaching backward and rolling from toe to heel as body weight transfers onto the rearward foot, trunk staying tall
rom: repeated reversed gait cycle — hip extension driving the reach-back with knee flexion/extension and toe-to-heel foot roll, cycling continuously through the stride

startPosition: standing or sitting tall, arms extended forward at chest/shoulder height, holding a resistance band with both hands palms down at shoulder-width, band taut
endPosition: arms swept out horizontally to the sides with the band drawn to the chest, shoulder blades fully retracted, elbows still soft-straight
rom: shoulder horizontal abduction from ~0° (arms forward) to ~90° (arms out to sides) driven by scapular retraction

startPosition: standing in a split-stance lunge with a resistance band looped around the distal tibia of the front leg pulling posteriorly, front foot flat with heel anchored, hands on a wall for support
endPosition: front knee driven forward past the toes toward the wall while the heel stays glued to the floor and the band drags the tibia anteriorly (facilitated posterior talar glide)
rom: ankle dorsiflexion from neutral to end-range knee-over-toe, isolated at the talocrural joint line

startPosition: supine on a flat bench, eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor, shoulder blades retracted and depressed into the bench, bar held at arms' length over the chest with a grip slightly wider than shoulder width
endPosition: bar lowered under control to the mid-chest with elbows flexed and wrists stacked over the elbows, at the deepest point before pressing back to full elbow extension
rom: full elbow flexion-to-extension with horizontal shoulder ad/abduction — bar travels from mid-chest to arms-length lockout overhead

startPosition: standing, feet hip-width, hip-hinged to roughly 30-45° of trunk lean (or near-parallel) with a neutral spine, gripping the barbell just outside the knees at arms' length
endPosition: bar pulled to the lower ribs/upper abdomen with elbows driven back and shoulder blades squeezed together, trunk angle held frozen
rom: elbow flexion combined with scapular retraction and shoulder extension — bar travels from arms-length to lower ribs while trunk inclination stays fixed

startPosition: quadruped with hands under the shoulders, toes tucked, and knees hovering about 2-5 cm off the floor, spine neutral and hips level with the shoulders
endPosition: having crawled forward by moving one hand and the opposite foot together in a small step, knees still hovering low, spine neutral and hips level with no rotation or hike
rom: reciprocal shoulder and hip flexion-extension in small steps with the knees hovering; the spine and pelvis hold near-still (anti-rotation isometric of the trunk)

startPosition: standing, feet hip-width with soft knees, hip-hinged to roughly 30-45° of trunk inclination with a neutral spine, arms hanging holding a barbell or dumbbells
endPosition: load pulled toward the lower ribs/upper abdomen with elbows driven back toward the pockets and shoulder blades squeezed together, trunk angle unchanged
rom: elbow flexion with scapular retraction and shoulder extension — load travels from full hang to lower ribs while the hip-hinge position is held constant

startPosition: standing tall with a quiet trunk, upper arms pinned to the ribs, holding a dumbbell or band with the elbow in full extension at the side
endPosition: forearm curled up to full elbow flexion with the weight near the front of the shoulder, elbow still pinned to the ribs
rom: elbow flexion from ~0° (full extension) to ~140° (full flexion), isolated with the upper arm stationary

startPosition: standing or seated tall, a dumbbell in each hand with arms at the sides, palms facing forward, elbows extended and upper arms pinned to the torso
endPosition: elbows bent to raise the dumbbells toward the shoulders at full flexion, wrists straight and trunk still (no swing or shrug)
rom: elbow flexion from ~0° to ~140° with the upper arms held against the torso throughout

startPosition: quadruped with hands directly under the shoulders and knees under the hips, spine neutral and deep core gently engaged
endPosition: opposite arm reaching forward and opposite leg extended back, both level with the torso in one long line from fingertip to heel, low back level and pelvis square
rom: shoulder flexion toward ~180° and hip extension to horizontal, while the lumbar spine and pelvis hold still (anti-rotation isometric of the trunk)

startPosition: quadruped with hands under the shoulders and knees under the hips, abdominals braced and spine neutral as if balancing a glass of water on the low back
endPosition: opposite elbow and knee drawn together beneath the body with the spine gently rounded and an exhale, having first extended that arm forward and leg back to torso height
rom: dynamic cycling between full opposite-limb extension (arm forward/leg back to torso level) and elbow-to-knee flexion under the body; pelvis kept square throughout

startPosition: supine and fully supported, body relaxed with arms resting at the sides, attention ready to move sequentially through body regions (feet → legs → pelvis → trunk → arms → head)
endPosition: same relaxed supine posture, pausing at each region for 30-60 s of interoceptive attention followed by a gentle micro-movement of that region (toe wiggle, ankle circle, pelvic rock)
rom: minimal — small isolated micro-movements at each region within a largely still, restful supine hold (near-isometric)

startPosition: standing tall, feet hip-width with knees softly unlocked, optionally holding a broomstick vertically along the spine touching the back of the head, mid-back, and tailbone
endPosition: hips pushed straight back with the chest tipped forward like a drawbridge, a neutral spine preserved (all three broomstick contact points maintained), a gentle hamstring stretch, and only slight knee bend
rom: hip flexion via posterior hip translation to roughly 70-90° of trunk incline with minimal lumbar motion and only a few degrees of knee flexion — a hinge, not a squat

startPosition: Seated upright, spine tall, feet flat on the floor, hands resting on the thighs, breathing quietly through the nose
endPosition: Same tall seated posture sustained through all four equal phases (4s inhale, 4s hold, 4s exhale, 4s hold), holds relaxed rather than strained
rom: Isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; only cyclic rib-cage and diaphragm expansion/recoil

startPosition: Standing tall facing a box, feet hip-width, then dipping into a quarter-squat countermovement with hips back and ankles, knees and hips flexed
endPosition: Landing softly on top of the box in a partial squat, hips back, knees tracking over the toes, ankles-knees-hips flexed to absorb the impact
rom: Full triple extension of ankles, knees and hips at takeoff, returning to flexed shock-absorption on landing

startPosition: Supine, knees bent ~90°, feet flat hip-width on the floor, arms at the sides
endPosition: Hips lifted so knees, hips and shoulders form a straight line, glutes squeezed with a slight posterior pelvic tilt, low back quiet
rom: 0–~30° hip extension from the floor up to a neutral straight-line bridge

startPosition: Seated upright, string held from the bridge of the nose to a fixed anchor point, gaze fixating the nearest bead
endPosition: Gaze converged sequentially onto each bead along the string so the fixated bead appears single with two strings crossing through it (physiological diplopia of the others)
rom: No joint motion — ocular vergence and accommodation excursion only; head held still

startPosition: Standing tall, feet hip-width, arms at the sides
endPosition: Explosive vertical jump with arms reaching overhead, landing softly through the whole foot with knees tracking over the toes before flowing into the next rep
rom: Full-body cyclic excursion each rep — standing to deep squat to rigid prone plank (optional push-up) back to an overhead jump

startPosition: Standing tall, feet hip-width (progressing to single-leg with load), forefoot on the floor or step edge with heels down
endPosition: Up on the balls of the feet at full ankle plantarflexion, ankles fully extended
rom: 0° to full ankle plantarflexion — 1s explosive concentric rise, 3s controlled eccentric lower

startPosition: Standing facing a wall, hands on the wall, one foot forward with the knee bent and the back leg straight with the heel flat
endPosition: Chest leaned toward the wall with the rear heel driven into the floor and the rear knee locked straight, gastrocnemius at end-range length
rom: Rear-leg ankle dorsiflexion to comfortable end range — sustained static hold

startPosition: Quadruped, hands under the shoulders, knees under the hips, spine neutral
endPosition: Alternating between full spinal extension (belly drops toward floor, tailbone lifts) and full spinal flexion (spine rounded to the ceiling, tailbone tucked)
rom: Full available thoracolumbar flexion-to-extension excursion, moved one segment at a time

startPosition: Begins seated (Stage 1) performing eye, head and shoulder movements, advancing to standing then walking as tolerated
endPosition: Progresses through standing trunk movements, gait with head turns, slopes and stairs eyes open/closed, to functional eye-hand coordination tasks
rom: Graded, progressively fuller eye, head, trunk and whole-body movement across seated, standing and gait stages

startPosition: Supine with a foam roller or towel roll placed across the cervicothoracic junction, knees bent, feet flat
endPosition: Chest draped open over the roll into thoracic extension, combined with active cervical range of motion (flexion, rotation)
rom: Thoracic extension over the roll plus full active cervical ROM

startPosition: Standing tall beside or behind a sturdy chair, fingertips resting lightly on the chair back, weight on both feet
endPosition: Weight shifted onto one leg with the opposite foot lifted just off the floor, hips level, holding a steady single-leg stance with a soft standing knee
rom: Isometric single-leg stance — minimal joint excursion; hip and ankle sway-control only

startPosition: Seated at the front edge of a sturdy chair, hands gripping the edge beside the hips, hips slid off the seat, arms straight supporting bodyweight
endPosition: Elbows bent to about 90° (upper arms roughly parallel to the floor) at the bottom, then pressed back to full elbow extension at the top
rom: ~0–90° elbow flexion/extension, elbows pointing straight back

startPosition: Supine on a flat bench, dumbbells held above the chest with palms facing each other and a soft fixed bend in the elbows
endPosition: Arms lowered in a wide arc to about bench level until a gentle stretch is felt across the chest, then squeezed back together over the mid-chest along the same arc
rom: Shoulder horizontal abduction to a chest-level stretch and back to horizontal adduction over the chest, elbow angle held constant throughout

startPosition: kneeling with shins on the floor, sit-bones settling back toward the heels, torso folded forward over the thighs, forehead resting on the floor or a support, arms reaching forward or alongside the body
endPosition: same folded kneeling fold sustained, abdomen resting against the thighs, breath directed into the posterior/lower rib cage — no change in body position
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; movement limited to posterior rib-cage and abdominal expansion during breathing

startPosition: seated tall with a neutral spine, head in neutral, two fingers placed lightly on the chin
endPosition: chin drawn straight back into craniocervical retraction/flexion until the back of the neck lengthens, held ~3 s with gentle finger overpressure
rom: cervical retraction (craniocervical flexion) from neutral to comfortable end range, no downward nod

startPosition: full dead hang from an overhead bar, supinated (palms-toward-you) grip about shoulder-width, arms fully extended, shoulder blades relaxed, legs quiet
endPosition: body pulled up until the chin clears the bar, elbows fully flexed toward the ribs, shoulder blades depressed and retracted, ribs down
rom: full elbow flexion with shoulder extension/adduction from dead hang to chin-over-bar, then controlled return to full extension

startPosition: sidelying with hips stacked vertically, hips flexed ~45°, knees bent ~90°, feet together, trunk neutral
endPosition: top knee rotated open toward the ceiling via hip external rotation while feet stay in contact and the pelvis stays stable (no posterior roll)
rom: hip external rotation, roughly 0 to ~30–45° of knee separation, then controlled return

startPosition: prone with legs extended, tops of the feet on the mat, palms flat under the shoulders, elbows tucked close to the ribs
endPosition: chest lifted into spinal extension leading with the sternum, elbows softly bent, shoulders drawn down and back, pubic bone and feet pressing into the floor
rom: lumbar and thoracic spinal extension from prone-flat to comfortable end-range back-bend, then controlled lowering

startPosition: supine with knees bent and feet flat on the floor hip-width apart, arms resting on the abdomen or out to the sides, whole body passively supported by the floor
endPosition: same supported supine position sustained with muscular guarding released; attention directed to interoceptive sensation — no active movement
rom: passive/isometric rest — minimal joint excursion

startPosition: seated upright holding a small near target (pencil tip or letter card) at arm's length at eye level, with a background card visible behind it
endPosition: target slowly drawn toward the bridge of the nose until it just doubles (near point of convergence), eyes maximally converged
rom: ocular convergence from arm's length to the near point of convergence; no somatic joint movement

startPosition: supine with both knees bent and feet flat, then one knee pulled firmly to the chest with both hands to lock that hemipelvis
endPosition: hips lifted by pressing through the grounded heel toward a straight knee-hip-shoulder line, pelvis kept level with no rotation
rom: limited hip extension from the floor — a low partial bridge kept short of the point where the pelvis would tilt or the hamstring cramps

startPosition: sidelying with the body straight, top leg supported on a bench at about hip height, bottom leg hanging below toward the floor, forearm/hand supporting the upper body
endPosition: body raised into a straight plank line by driving the bottom inner thigh up toward the bench via hip adduction, trunk and legs aligned crown-to-heel
rom: hip adduction lifting the pelvis/trunk from the lowered hanging position up to a straight body line, then a slow ~3 s eccentric descent

startPosition: half-kneeling with the rear shin vertical against a wall or couch (rear foot elevated, knee flexed), front foot planted flat, torso upright
endPosition: rear hip driven into extension with a posterior pelvic tilt while the rear knee stays flexed and the front knee travels forward, chest lifted tall — maximal biarticular rectus femoris/hip-flexor lengthening
rom: combined hip extension and knee flexion to comfortable end-range anterior thigh and hip-flexor stretch, held statically

startPosition: standing and leaning forward with both hands on a countertop edge slightly wider than shoulder-width, feet walked back so the body forms a straight, gently inclined line from head to heels
endPosition: chest lowered toward the counter with elbows bent ~45° to the torso, then pressed back to full elbow extension with the shoulder blades protracting at the top
rom: full elbow flexion-to-extension lowering the chest to the counter and pressing back up (inclined push-up range), trunk held rigid throughout

startPosition: standing facing a sturdy countertop, feet shoulder-width apart, hands resting lightly on the edge for balance, trunk tall
endPosition: hips pushed back and knees bent to a comfortable squat depth with knees tracking over the second/third toes, then driven back up through the heels to full standing with glutes squeezed
rom: combined hip and knee flexion to comfortable (as-tolerated) squat depth and return to standing extension

startPosition: prone with the forehead resting on stacked hands, legs extended, the whole front of the body in contact with the floor
endPosition: same prone position sustained; the belly presses down into the floor and the low back rises slightly on inhale, then settles on exhale — no change in body position
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; movement limited to abdominal and lower-rib expansion against the floor during breathing

startPosition: prone, legs extended, forehead resting on stacked hands, whole body relaxed into the floor
endPosition: same prone position sustained; on inhale the belly and lower ribs press down into the floor and the low back rises slightly, releasing on exhale
rom: isometric respiratory posture — minimal joint excursion; movement is diaphragmatic/lower-rib expansion with slight lumbar rise and fall

startPosition: standing or seated tall, working arm raised to ~90° and brought across the front of the body, opposite hand cradling the elbow
endPosition: working arm pulled horizontally across the chest toward the opposite shoulder, shoulder blade pinned down, held at comfortable end range
rom: glenohumeral horizontal adduction to end range (~stretch across posterior shoulder), scapula stabilized

startPosition: supine, knees bent ~90°, feet flat on floor, hands lightly behind head or crossed on chest, low back in contact with floor
endPosition: head and shoulder blades curled off the floor, ribs drawn toward the pelvis, low back still in contact
rom: upper trunk/thoracic flexion, lifting scapulae off the floor (~30° trunk flexion), stopping well short of a full sit-up

startPosition: seated upright, spine tall, shoulders relaxed, breathing quietly through the nose
endPosition: same seated posture; a double nasal inhale (short inhale then a second stacking inhale to fill the lungs) followed by a prolonged exhale through the mouth, repeated
rom: isometric seated posture — minimal joint excursion; excursion is full-then-topped-off inhalation and extended exhalation of the rib cage

startPosition: supine, knees bent or legs extended, head neutral, pressure biofeedback cuff behind the neck inflated to baseline (~20 mmHg)
endPosition: gentle craniocervical nod (chin tucked, back of neck lengthened) raising cuff pressure toward 22–30 mmHg and held ~10 s without superficial neck-flexor substitution
rom: small craniocervical flexion (a subtle head nod), held isometrically at graded pressure targets

startPosition: standing on top of a box, feet hip-width, ready to step off
endPosition: after a brief stiff, springy ground contact on landing, immediately rebounding into a maximal vertical jump, arms driving upward
rom: drop from box then rapid stretch-shortening cycle — brief eccentric knee/hip/ankle flexion on landing followed by explosive triple extension into vertical take-off

startPosition: standing or seated at a desk, elbow straight, back of the hand flat on the desk with palm up and fingers pointing toward the body
endPosition: body leaning to press the hand down and draw the wrist toward flexion, fingers softly curled, held at a mild stretch over the top of the forearm
rom: wrist flexion to comfortable end range with elbow extended, lengthening the wrist/finger extensors

startPosition: seated tall at a desk, elbow straight, palm flat on the desktop with fingers pointing back toward the body
endPosition: trunk leaned away from the fixed hand to draw the wrist into extension, held at a mild stretch along the palm-side forearm
rom: wrist extension to comfortable end range with elbow extended, lengthening the wrist/finger flexors

startPosition: seated at a table, pegboard and pegs within reach, forearm supported, fingers ready in a precision pinch
endPosition: individual pegs picked up with a thumb–index pinch and placed one at a time into target holes, repeated at a controlled accurate pace
rom: fine distal motion — thumb/finger pinch-and-release with small wrist and forearm positioning movements; minimal proximal joint excursion

startPosition: supine, knees bent or legs extended, one hand on the belly and one on the lower ribs (optionally a band around the lower ribs)
endPosition: same relaxed posture; on inhale the belly rises first and the lower ribs spread laterally, followed by a slow pursed-lip exhale
rom: isometric supine posture — minimal joint excursion; excursion is diaphragmatic descent and 360° lower-rib expansion with each breath

startPosition: supine, hands on the lower abdomen and lateral ribs, spine neutral
endPosition: same posture; inhale expands the trunk 360° (front, sides, back), then on exhale the abdominal wall gently braces to ~30% effort while breathing continues around the brace
rom: isometric supine posture — minimal joint excursion; excursion is 360° rib/abdominal expansion with a sustained low-level abdominal brace

startPosition: upright, suspended between parallel bars, elbows locked and shoulders depressed, supporting full bodyweight on the hands
endPosition: elbows bent and shoulders extended so the upper arms reach roughly parallel to the floor at the bottom, then pressed back to full elbow extension
rom: shoulder extension with elbow flexion lowering to ~90° elbow / upper arms parallel to floor, then full return to elbow lockout

startPosition: standing facing a doorway, both hands on the frame at chest height, one leg stepped back with heel flat and back knee straight
endPosition: hips leaned toward the doorframe with the back heel kept flat, held at a stretch through the back of the calf (back knee softened slightly to shift lower to the Achilles/soleus)
rom: ankle dorsiflexion to comfortable end range with knee extended (gastrocnemius bias) or slightly flexed (soleus/Achilles bias)

startPosition: standing tall in the center of an open doorway, both hands gripping opposite sides of the doorframe at waist-to-lower-rib height, elbows bent ~90°, ribs stacked down over pelvis
endPosition: same braced rowing posture sustained — shoulder blades pulled down and back, elbows driving backward into the immovable frame, no trunk lean or shrug
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; elbows held at ~90° throughout while pulling against the fixed frame

startPosition: standing at an open doorway, one hand gripping the frame at or just above shoulder height with the elbow fully straight, feet slightly staggered
endPosition: hips hinged back and down away from the anchored arm so the shoulder sinks into overhead flexion, a broad stretch felt down the side of the back and axilla
rom: shoulder flexion / latissimus lengthening driven by hip hinge to a firm, comfortable end range

startPosition: standing in a doorway with the forearm resting against the frame, shoulder abducted to 45°, 90°, or 135° (depending on the portion targeted) and elbow bent ~90°
endPosition: back foot stepped forward through the doorway so the trunk moves ahead of the fixed arm, opening the front of the shoulder and chest with the sternum rotating outward
rom: shoulder horizontal abduction / pectoralis lengthening to a firm end range (no anterior shoulder pinch)

startPosition: quadruped with hands under the shoulders, fingers spread and rooted, toes tucked under
endPosition: hips lifted up and back into an inverted V, arms and spine forming one long line, sit bones reaching toward the ceiling, heels sinking toward the floor, head relaxed between the upper arms
rom: sustained hold — overhead shoulder flexion, deep hip flexion, and knee/ankle extension taken to a neutral-spine end range

startPosition: supine on a flat bench, feet flat on the floor, shoulder blades set back and down into the bench, a dumbbell held at each side of the chest with wrists stacked over the elbows
endPosition: both dumbbells pressed upward and slightly together until the elbows are extended, weights stacked over the shoulders
rom: from ~90° elbow flexion / upper arms near bench level (comfortable chest stretch) to full elbow extension with horizontal shoulder adduction

startPosition: seated upright (or standing) with a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, forearms vertical, palms forward, ribcage stacked over the pelvis
endPosition: both dumbbells pressed overhead to full elbow extension, weights stacked directly over the shoulders without low-back arch
rom: shoulder flexion/abduction from ~90° (ear level) to full overhead elevation with elbow extension to lockout

startPosition: standing upright at the start of a straight walking path, eyes fixed on a target ahead
endPosition: mid-gait with the head rotated horizontally (progressing to vertical) on a set cadence while maintaining a steady, unstaggered straight-line walking path
rom: cervical rotation to a comfortable end range every 2–3 steps, coordinated with continuous forward gait

startPosition: standing with a resistance band or cable anchored behind, arms reaching forward at ~90° shoulder flexion, elbows soft, shoulder blades protracted
endPosition: arms horizontally adducted across the body until the hands meet in front (as if hugging a large tree), scapulae fully protracted around the ribs
rom: horizontal shoulder adduction from arms-wide to hands meeting at the midline, emphasizing scapular protraction

startPosition: standing tall at the top of a staircase, hand resting lightly on the handrail, weight loaded onto the top-step (trailing) leg
endPosition: trailing knee bent slowly under control to lower the front foot softly onto the step below, most of the load absorbed by the supporting leg's quadriceps
rom: controlled eccentric knee flexion of the supporting leg through one step's height (~15–20 cm) over roughly 3–4 seconds

startPosition: standing on a step on the working leg, pelvis level and trunk tall, the non-working leg hanging off the edge
endPosition: working knee bent slowly to lower the opposite heel toward the floor for a light tap, then a return to the start
rom: eccentric knee flexion of the stance leg through step height (progressing 4" → 8" → 12") over ~3 seconds down

startPosition: standing with arms at the sides, thumbs pointing down (internal rotation), positioned in the scapular plane ~30° forward of the frontal plane
endPosition: arms elevated in the scapular plane to shoulder height with thumbs still down, as if pouring water from a can toward the floor
rom: scapular-plane elevation from 0° to ~90° (shoulder height, no higher), shoulder held in internal rotation

startPosition: side-lying with the working arm on top, elbow bent 90° and tucked to the side over a towel roll, forearm resting across the abdomen
endPosition: forearm rotated outward and upward to point away from the body, the elbow staying pinned at the side and the towel trapped throughout
rom: glenohumeral external rotation from ~0° (hand across the belly) to a comfortable end range, arm kept at 0° abduction

startPosition: seated at a screen with the near, fixated visual workspace in front
endPosition: gaze shifted to a specific target at least 20 feet away and held there for at least 20 seconds with a few full blinks
rom: ocular accommodation and vergence relaxation to distance fixation — minimal joint excursion, no gross body movement

startPosition: seated upright, eyes closed, palms briskly rubbed together until warm
endPosition: warm palms lightly cupped over the closed eyes without touching or pressing the eyeballs, breathing slowly
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; sustained cupped-hand posture over the eyes

startPosition: standing tall facing a face-height cable/band anchor, arms extended forward gripping the handles with palms down or neutral, ribs stacked over pelvis
endPosition: handles pulled to the face with elbows wide and high, forearms externally rotated so knuckles point toward the ceiling and thumbs toward the ears, shoulder blades squeezed together
rom: elbow flexion 0–~90°+ with shoulder horizontal abduction and external rotation to full scapular retraction

startPosition: standing tall with a heavy weight held in each hand at the sides, ribs packed and trunk braced, crushing the handles
endPosition: same erect braced posture maintained while marching forward along a straight line for the prescribed distance or time
rom: isometric trunk (anti-lateral-flexion, minimal spinal excursion) combined with reciprocal gait

startPosition: seated with the hand resting and fingers held long and straight, a card/coin/thin object placed between two adjacent fingers
endPosition: adjacent fingers squeezed together to clamp the card so it cannot be pulled out, pressure held ~5 s
rom: finger adduction — small MCP adduction excursion to closed-gap, then largely isometric hold

startPosition: seated with the hand resting and fingers extended straight, a folded paper placed between two adjacent fingers
endPosition: fingers pinched toward the middle finger to clamp the paper, held ~5 s before release
rom: finger adduction — small MCP adduction excursion to closed-gap, then isometric hold

startPosition: seated with the hand resting, wrist straight, a rubber band looped around all five fingertips with fingers and thumb drawn together
endPosition: fingers and thumb spread wide apart against the band's resistance, open shape held ~2 s
rom: full finger and thumb abduction/extension from closed to maximally spread against resistance

startPosition: standing on a compliant foam surface in a Romberg stance (feet together), eyes open, near a wall or counter for safety
endPosition: steady upright stance held while progressing the challenge — eyes closed or busy visual background, and narrowing base from Romberg to tandem to single-leg
rom: isometric stance — minimal joint excursion; postural-sway control at the ankle/hip

startPosition: supine with knees bent, heels resting on a foam roller, arms at the sides
endPosition: hips lifted so knees, hips, and shoulders form a straight line, knees held hip-width
rom: 0–~full hip extension, lifting the pelvis from the floor to the bridged straight-line position

startPosition: seated with the elbow braced on the thigh, forearm in neutral holding a weighted hammer or dowel vertically
endPosition: hammer head rotated fully toward the floor (pronation) and toward the ceiling (supination), elbow staying pinned to the thigh
rom: full forearm pronation-to-supination, ~180° combined rotation about the forearm axis

startPosition: standing tall with feet hip-width, trunk erect
endPosition: split-stance lunge with the front shin vertical and the back knee lowered toward the floor, trunk upright
rom: front hip and knee flexion ~0–90° with trailing-hip extension; trunk translates straight down then straight up

startPosition: seated upright and relaxed
endPosition: same seated posture sustained through the 4-count nasal inhale, 7-count hold, and slow 8-count mouth exhale cycle
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; movement limited to rib-cage/diaphragm respiratory excursion

startPosition: quadruped kneeling with knees spread wide, feet turned outward, and weight supported on the hands or forearms
endPosition: hips sunk down toward the floor between the wide knees into a deep sustained adductor stretch, spine long
rom: hip abduction to comfortable end range, held statically while gravity slowly widens the knees

startPosition: prone on the forearms with elbows under the shoulders and toes tucked
endPosition: body lifted into one straight line from crown to heel, tailbone tucked and shoulders packed, held isometrically
rom: isometric anti-extension hold — minimal joint excursion; trunk maintains a neutral straight line against gravity

startPosition: standing tall, barbell in front-rack across the anterior deltoids/clavicles with elbows high (clean or crossed-arm grip), feet shoulder-width, toes turned out 10–30°, trunk upright and braced
endPosition: bottom of squat with hips at or below knee level, torso upright, elbows still driven high, then driven back up to full standing
rom: 0 to ~120°+ knee flexion with matching deep hip flexion; trunk stays vertical

startPosition: standing tall, working arm resting at the side, elbow straight, thumb pointing up
endPosition: arm elevated in the scapular plane (30–45° anterior to the coronal plane) to shoulder height with thumb up ('pouring water toward the ceiling'), upper trap staying soft
rom: 0–90° shoulder elevation in the scapular plane

startPosition: supine, knees bent ~90°, feet flat hip-width, hips lifted into a bridge so knees–hips–shoulders form a straight line and the pelvis is level
endPosition: one knee marched up toward the chest to ~90° hip flexion while the hips stay level (no pelvic rocking), then lowered and alternated
rom: support hip held in bridge extension; marching hip flexes 0–~90°; pelvis excursion held near zero (anti-rotation)

startPosition: standing, kettlebell/dumbbell held vertically against the chest with both hands, feet shoulder-width or slightly wider, toes turned out 10–30°
endPosition: bottom of squat with elbows tracking just inside the knees (or comfortable depth), trunk tall and weight glued to the chest, then driven back to standing
rom: 0 to ~120° knee flexion with matching hip flexion; upright trunk throughout

startPosition: seated, forearm supported, wrist neutral to slightly extended, hand open with fingers relaxed (optional stress ball in palm)
endPosition: firm full fist with thumb wrapped, held 3 s, then hand fully opened with fingers spread wide
rom: full finger MCP/IP flexion to full extension; wrist held neutral

startPosition: seated, forearm supported, wrist neutral, fingers extended around a therapy ball or putty
endPosition: full finger flexion crushing the ball into a smaller shape, then fingers spread wide on release
rom: full finger flexion to full extension; wrist held neutral

startPosition: standing, a dumbbell in each hand at the sides in neutral grip (palms facing thighs, thumbs up), elbows fully extended and pinned to the ribs
endPosition: elbows flexed to curl the weights toward the shoulders with thumbs still up, then lowered over ~3 s to full extension
rom: 0 to ~140° elbow flexion; forearm held in neutral rotation

startPosition: vertical dead hang from an overhead bar with overhand grip, shoulders packed down away from the ears, legs straight and hanging
endPosition: straight (or near-straight) legs raised to at least hip height, finished with a posterior pelvic curl bringing the pelvis toward the ribs, then lowered under control
rom: 0 to ≥90° hip flexion plus posterior pelvic tilt at the top; no trunk swing

startPosition: seated upright holding a near letter chart at reading distance with a matched far letter chart placed across the room at distance
endPosition: gaze alternating between near and far charts, reading a row on each as quickly as possible while keeping the letters sharp
rom: isometric posture — ocular accommodation/vergence shifts near-to-far with minimal head or body excursion

startPosition: standing with a loaded barbell across the shoulders, balls of the feet on a platform edge and heels dropped below it into dorsiflexion (straight-knee variant; bent-knee/leg-press variants biasing soleus)
endPosition: full ankle plantarflexion up onto the toes, then a slow controlled lowering back below the platform, ~6 s per rep
rom: below-neutral dorsiflexion (heel dropped) to full plantarflexion under heavy slow load

startPosition: standing tall, feet flat hip-width, knees soft, weight balanced over the big and second toes
endPosition: risen up onto the balls of the feet with heels fully lifted into plantarflexion, crown reaching toward the ceiling
rom: 0 to full ankle plantarflexion

startPosition: standing on a step edge, up on the balls of the feet with heels raised (top of the calf raise), knee locked long
endPosition: heels lowered slowly over ~4 s to just below the step edge into dorsiflexion, weight even across the toes (bilateral progressing to unilateral)
rom: full plantarflexion to below-neutral dorsiflexion, eccentrically controlled

startPosition: standing tall on a floor line (tile seam or tape), feet aligned heel-to-toe, arms held slightly out, gaze fixed on a spot ahead, optional light fingertip wall support
endPosition: front-foot heel placed directly in front of and touching the back-foot toes on the single line, pausing in each narrow tandem stance before the next step
rom: dynamic tandem gait along a line with a very narrow base of support; minimal individual-joint excursion per step

startPosition: standing tall at one end of a straight line/taped seam, feet together, arms relaxed at sides or slightly abducted for balance
endPosition: advanced several steps forward in tandem, heel of the front foot placed directly in contact with the toes of the trailing foot, on a very narrow base
rom: whole-body forward locomotion along a straight line, each step ~one foot-length; individual joints move little — emphasis is on progressively narrowing the base of support

startPosition: standing tall on the support leg, opposite foot resting on a slider directly beneath the hip, hand on the pelvis to monitor level hips
endPosition: sliding foot pushed out into hip abduction and/or back into hip extension, then drawn back under control with the standing pelvis kept level
rom: hip abduction ~0–30° and/or hip extension ~0–20° from neutral, moved through the full arc with a deliberately slow eccentric return

startPosition: sidelying with the bottom leg slightly bent for a base, top leg straight and stacked over the bottom, pelvis vertical, hand on the top hip
endPosition: top leg raised into hip abduction with the heel leading toward the ceiling and the knee tracking in line with the trunk
rom: hip abduction ~0–30–45° from neutral, controlled lift and lower

startPosition: sidelying with the top leg bent forward onto a support, bottom leg straight along the floor, hips stacked vertically
endPosition: bottom leg lifted off the floor toward the ceiling into hip adduction, heel rising toward the top thigh, leg kept straight and slightly forward of the trunk
rom: hip adduction ~0–20–30° (floor to comfortable end range), controlled up and down

startPosition: single-leg stance with the knee slightly bent, trunk hinged forward at the hip to near-horizontal, non-stance leg extended behind in line with the trunk
endPosition: trunk and pelvis rotated from the hip toward the stance side (internal rotation) then away (external rotation), returning to a level near-horizontal position
rom: hip internal/external rotation to comfortable end range under single-leg load, with the trunk held in ~90° hip-hinge and the spine kept still

startPosition: half-kneeling with the front foot flat, rear knee on the floor, pelvis in posterior tilt and trunk tall (hip flexor); or supine with a strap around one foot (hamstring)
endPosition: hip flexor lengthened by driving the front knee forward over the foot into rear-hip extension; hamstring lengthened by raising the strapped straight leg toward the ceiling
rom: rear-hip extension to a comfortable stretch; supine straight-leg raise toward ~80–90° hip flexion, each sustained at end range

startPosition: standing with feet hip-width, neutral spine, soft knees, load (bar/light weight) hanging at the thighs or set at mid-shin
endPosition: hips pushed back then driven forward to a tall, fully extended standing position with the load lifted along the legs and glutes squeezed
rom: hip flexion-to-extension ~90°→0° via a hip-dominant hinge with a neutral spine and minimal knee bend

startPosition: seated on the floor with the upper back against a bench, knees bent ~90°, feet flat hip-width apart, chin tucked and ribs down
endPosition: hips driven up until the trunk and thighs are parallel to the floor (tabletop), shins vertical, hips fully extended with a strong glute squeeze and slight posterior pelvic tilt
rom: hip extension from a flexed bottom position (~70° flexion) up to neutral/0° at the tabletop

startPosition: supine on the floor, low back flattened into the ground, arms reaching overhead and legs straight together
endPosition: shoulder blades, arms, and straight legs lifted a few inches into a dished, banana-shaped position and held isometrically
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; the low back is kept pinned flat to resist lumbar extension (anti-extension)

startPosition: seated with the forearm supported, wrist neutral, hand and small finger relaxed
endPosition: 5th metacarpal flexed (cupped) with the small finger flexed to lock around a handle, or with the small finger extended to support a plate
rom: 5th CMC/MCP flexion paired with small-finger flexion or extension to end range, held largely isometrically in each grip pattern

startPosition: seated with the forearm supported and the hand relaxed
endPosition: MCP joints flexed 60–90° while the IP joints are held fully extended, forming a flat 'tabletop' hand, sustained for the hold
rom: isometric hold at MCP flexion 60–90° with full IP extension; minimal joint excursion once the position is set

startPosition: supine under a fixed bar or suspension trainer, arms fully extended gripping the handle, body in a straight rigid plank supported on the heels
endPosition: body pulled up as one plank until the chest nears the bar, elbows driven back toward the pockets, shoulder blades retracted
rom: elbow flexion 0°→~120° with scapular retraction while the trunk-and-legs plank rises as a unit

startPosition: seated with the forearm and hand supported, fingers extended and the small finger held adducted alongside the ring finger, a rubber band or manual resistance across the finger
endPosition: small finger abducted sideways away from the ring finger against the resistance and held ~3 s before controlled return
rom: 5th-MCP abduction from neutral to end range and back, isolated to the small finger

startPosition: seated upright (or standing) with feet flat, spine tall, glutes relaxed
endPosition: both gluteal muscles maximally contracted and held ~5 s with no visible movement of the pelvis or hips
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; no visible hip or pelvic motion

startPosition: seated tall with a neutral cervical spine, a hand/palm placed against the forehead, side, or back of the head
endPosition: cervical muscles contract to match a steady manual resistance in flexion, extension, lateral flexion, and rotation while the head stays still, upper traps quiet
rom: isometric hold — minimal to no cervical joint excursion in each of the four directions

startPosition: standing with feet shoulder-width apart, arms ready to swing back
endPosition: full triple extension of ankles, knees, and hips at the peak of an explosive vertical jump, arms thrown overhead, then a soft landing reabsorbed into the same squat depth
rom: quarter-to-half squat (~0–60° knee flexion) driving into full hip/knee/ankle extension and airborne, then re-flexing to absorb the landing

startPosition: standing tall with feet together and arms at the sides
endPosition: feet jumped out wider than hip-width with straight arms swung out and overhead (hands near touching), landing softly on the balls of the feet
rom: rhythmic shoulder abduction ~0–180° with hip abduction/adduction; feet cycle from together to wider than hip-width and back

startPosition: standing with feet hip-width, kettlebell held at the chest (goblet) or behind the neck, spine long and neutral
endPosition: trunk hinged forward toward horizontal with the hips pushed back, then driven back to a tall standing finish with hips fully extended
rom: hip-dominant hinge to roughly torso-near-parallel (~0–90° hip flexion) with slightly bent knees and a fixed neutral spine throughout

startPosition: standing with feet shoulder-width, hips hinged and the kettlebell hiked back between the legs above knee height
endPosition: hips snapped to full extension with a tall trunk as the kettlebell floats out to chest/shoulder height on nearly straight arms
rom: dynamic hip hinge cycling from hip flexion (bell above the knees) to full hip extension; bell arcs from between the thighs up to ~chest height

startPosition: standing in a split stance lowered into a split squat with the front shin vertical, held at the target knee-flexion angle
endPosition: sustained isometric split-squat hold at the target knee flexion (progressing toward 45-second holds) with no movement, crown reaching tall
rom: isometric hold at the target knee-flexion angle — minimal joint excursion once the position is set

startPosition: standing facing a wall in a short lunge, front foot a few inches from the wall with the heel firmly planted, arch lifted
endPosition: front knee driven straight forward over the middle toes to touch (or reach toward) the wall, loading the ankle into dorsiflexion while the heel stays glued down
rom: ankle dorsiflexion to end range at the knee-to-wall position; heel remains grounded throughout

startPosition: standing with a braced tall trunk, the barbell anchored at one end and racked at the front of one shoulder in that hand
endPosition: bar pressed forward and up along its arc to full elbow extension, the working hand reaching toward the far upper corner, ribs kept down
rom: shoulder elevation through an arced path (roughly 40–120°+ elevation, avoiding the impingement zone) to full elbow extension

startPosition: seated with thighs secured under the pad, pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder width, arms fully extended overhead, torso leaning back 10–15° with chest lifted
endPosition: bar pulled down to the upper chest/collarbone by driving the elbows down and back and depressing the shoulder blades, then controlled back to full overhead extension
rom: shoulder adduction/extension from full overhead flexion to the bar at the upper chest, with elbow flexion through full range

startPosition: standing with a band looped around the ankles or just above the knees, dropped into a quarter-squat (mini-squat), feet hip-width and pointed forward
endPosition: stepped sideways one step while maintaining constant band tension and a low quarter-squat, hips level, followed by a controlled trailing-leg return step
rom: frontal-plane hip abduction and controlled adduction while sustaining ~quarter-squat knee flexion and a level pelvis

startPosition: single-leg stance with the hip and knee slightly flexed, trunk quiet, loaded to push off laterally
endPosition: explosive lateral push-off into flight, landing and sticking on the opposite single leg with the knee tracking over the toes, silently absorbed
rom: frontal-plane push-off and landing — hip/knee flexion to absorb then extension to propel, with lateral displacement from one leg to the other

startPosition: seated upright with hands resting on the lower lateral ribs, shoulders relaxed
endPosition: lower ribs expanded sideways into the hands on inhale without any chest lift or shoulder shrug, then settling back on exhale
rom: lateral rib-cage (costal) expansion through the breathing cycle — minimal limb or spinal joint excursion

startPosition: standing tall, feet hip-width, a dumbbell in each hand resting at the sides with a soft bend in the elbows, palms facing the thighs
endPosition: arms raised out to the sides in the frontal plane to about shoulder height with elbows leading, shoulders down (not shrugged), torso still
rom: 0–~90° shoulder abduction in the frontal plane, controlled up and down

startPosition: standing beside a step/box, tall trunk and level pelvis, the near (top) foot placed flat on the step and the trailing foot on the floor
endPosition: fully stood up on the step driving through the top-leg heel, standing hip and pelvis level, trailing foot lifted off the floor
rom: top leg moves from ~60–90° knee/hip flexion to near-full extension; controlled lowering back down

startPosition: prone on a leg-curl machine (or seated variation), hips and pelvis pressed into the pad, knees near full extension, padded lever resting just above the heels/Achilles, ankles relaxed/lightly dorsiflexed
endPosition: knees flexed with heels curled toward the buttocks through available range, hips still anchored to the pad, brief pause at end range
rom: ~0° to end-range knee flexion (up to ~90–120°), controlled return to near-full extension

startPosition: seated tall on a leg-extension machine, back and hips against the pads, hips flexed ~90°, shins hooked behind the roller resting just above the ankles, knee axis aligned with the machine pivot
endPosition: knees smoothly extended to nearly straight (short of hyperextended lockout) with quadriceps squeezed, brief pause
rom: ~90° knee flexion to near-full extension (~0–10° short of lockout), lowering over 2–3 s

startPosition: seated in a leg-press machine, long spine and quiet trunk against the back pad, both feet flat on the platform hip-width, knees near full extension at the start
endPosition: platform pressed away through both heels back toward extension after a controlled descent stopped at the 60° marker, knees kept hip-width
rom: 0–60° knee flexion only (limited depth to minimize patellofemoral stress)

startPosition: supine with buttocks close to the wall, straight legs resting vertically up the wall, arms relaxed at the sides, whole body passively supported
endPosition: sustained restorative hold with legs vertical and body fully relaxed, breathing slowly and low into the belly with lengthened exhales for 5–15 minutes
rom: isometric restorative hold — minimal joint excursion; hips passively flexed ~90° with legs vertical

startPosition: seated tall with a long spine, one hand can anchor the same-side shoulder down; head neutral
endPosition: neck flexed, side-bent away from the target side, and rotated toward the target side (looking down toward the opposite armpit), same-side shoulder drawn toward the floor to elongate levator scapulae
rom: cervical flexion + contralateral lateral flexion + ipsilateral rotation to comfortable end range, held statically

startPosition: seated comfortably and still, attention directed inward to a region of body comfort (e.g. warm feet, soft hands)
endPosition: attention oscillating between the comfort area and an area of mild discomfort/activation (e.g. tight shoulders), returning to comfort if overwhelming
rom: isometric/attentional practice — no joint excursion; 30–60 s of focused attention per area

startPosition: seated (or standing) tall with a long spine, arms extended forward holding a band or cable anchored at about waist height, scapulae protracted
endPosition: band/cable pulled toward the abdomen/belt buckle with elbows driven toward the back hip pockets and scapulae retracted/depressed, quiet upper traps
rom: full shoulder extension/scapular retraction pull to the abdomen, controlled return to forward reach

startPosition: supine with one knee bent (foot flat) and the other leg straight, hands placed palm-down under the lumbar spine to preserve the natural lordosis, abdominals braced
endPosition: head and shoulders lifted just off the surface with the lumbar curve maintained (no flexion), gentle pressure of the low back into the fingers, brief hold
rom: minimal spinal flexion — only head/upper-shoulder elevation off the floor; near-isometric trunk brace

startPosition: seated upright, working arm abducted at the shoulder with elbow flexed, wrist/fingers relaxed, head neutral
endPosition: sequenced glide: shoulder abducted, elbow extended, wrist/fingers extended (palm open toward ceiling), then cervical lateral flexion away from the arm — tension applied at one end while released at the other so the nerve slides
rom: oscillating neurodynamic slider through combined shoulder abduction, elbow extension, wrist/finger extension, and contralateral cervical side-bend; gentle, non-stretch excursion

startPosition: standing, entering a chosen static or dynamic stretch position, attention on the breath and target tissue, breathing slowly through the nose
endPosition: stretch held at the edge where sensation is clear but not painful for 20–30 s, then a slow mindful transition to the next pose with 10–15 s rest
rom: tissue-specific stretch to comfortable end range, held 20–30 s; slow controlled transitions between poses

startPosition: supine; for the curl-up one knee bent with hands under the lumbar spine, for the dead bug arms and legs raised to 90° with the low back pressed to the floor
endPosition: curl-up: head and shoulders lifted minimally with the lumbar curve held; dead bug: opposite arm and leg lowered toward the floor while lumbar contact and quiet ribs are maintained, then returned
rom: minimal spinal flexion in the curl-up; dead bug ranges through near-full opposite hip/shoulder extension while keeping the spine still (anti-extension control)

startPosition: high plank, hands stacked under shoulders on the floor, weight on hands and toes, body in a straight line from head to heels, hips level
endPosition: one knee driven toward the chest with the opposite leg extended, trunk rigid and hips level, then legs rapidly switch in a running motion
rom: repetitive hip flexion ~0–90° driving knee to chest, alternating; trunk held isometric in plank

startPosition: seated, forearm supported, wrist and fingers in neutral, shoulder at side, elbow flexed
endPosition: end of the glide sequence: for nerve glide, shoulder abducted with elbow, wrist and fingers extended and cervical spine laterally flexed away; for tendon glide, hand cycled through straight, hook fist, full fist, tabletop, and straight fist
rom: full available excursion of wrist/finger flexion–extension and elbow extension with graded cervical lateral flexion; gentle, non-provocative end ranges

startPosition: standing on the involved leg on a step or floor, pelvis level, kneecap tracking over the second toe, opposite foot lifted
endPosition: single-leg stance maintained through a slow step-down until the trailing heel lightly touches the floor, hip and pelvis staying level
rom: controlled single-leg knee flexion through the step-down descent (~0–45°) with proprioceptive/perturbation stance holds

startPosition: seated on the floor in 90/90 position — front leg 90° hip flexion and 90° knee flexion, back leg 90° hip abduction and 90° knee flexion, spine tall
endPosition: trunk hinged forward over the front shin with a long spine, feeling a stretch in the front-hip external rotators toward internal rotation
rom: front-hip external rotators lengthened toward end-range internal rotation via forward trunk lean; sustained stretch

startPosition: seated on the floor, both hips at 90° flexion, front leg in external rotation and back leg in internal rotation, both sit-bones anchored, spine tall
endPosition: both knees pressed toward the floor with chest reaching gently over the front shin, trunk stationary and sit-bones still anchored
rom: hip rotators lengthened to comfortable end range in front-hip ER and back-hip IR; sustained myofascial stretch

startPosition: tall kneeling with ankles anchored by a partner or strap, hips extended, trunk in a straight line from knees to crown, hands ready in front
endPosition: trunk lowered forward toward the floor under eccentric hamstring control, hands catching the floor, then pushing back up to tall kneeling
rom: knee extension from ~90° toward full extension controlled eccentrically over ~5 s with hips held extended

startPosition: tall kneeling with ankles anchored, hips extended, straight line from knees to crown
endPosition: body lowered forward as slowly as possible toward the floor under maximal eccentric hamstring control, hands catching and pushing back up
rom: eccentric knee extension from ~90° toward full extension, trunk/hips rigid, controlled as slowly as possible

startPosition: sidelying with hips and knees stacked at 90°, both arms extended together in front at shoulder height, knees pressed together
endPosition: top arm and thorax rotated open toward the ceiling and floor behind, gaze following the moving hand, pelvis and knees stationary
rom: thoracic rotation to comfortable end range (~30–40° cumulative) while pelvis is stabilized

startPosition: seated (or standing near a wall), eyes open and fixed on a repetitive moving visual pattern such as an optokinetic disc or scrolling video
endPosition: sustained visual fixation on the moving pattern for the exposure duration (start ~30 s), habituating to visual-vestibular conflict
rom: isometric head/body posture with eyes tracking optic flow — minimal joint excursion

startPosition: seated tall with back and glutes set against the bench, dumbbells or band held at shoulder height, wrists stacked over elbows, ribs down
endPosition: weights pressed straight overhead to full elbow extension with wrists stacked over elbows over shoulders
rom: shoulder flexion/abduction ~90° to overhead with full elbow extension

startPosition: standing hip-width, barbell or dowel held overhead with elbows locked and scapulae packed, bar over the mid-foot
endPosition: full squat with hips below parallel, arms still extended overhead, bar tracking vertically over the mid-foot, then standing to full extension
rom: hip flexion >110°, knee flexion to deep squat, ankle dorsiflexion ~36–38°, shoulder flexion >170° held overhead

startPosition: standing perpendicular to a cable or band anchor, handle held at the chest with both hands, hips square and trunk braced
endPosition: arms pressed straight forward to full extension while resisting the rotational pull, hips and trunk staying square and quiet
rom: arms extend/return at the shoulder ~0–90° elbow extension; trunk held isometric anti-rotation with minimal spinal excursion

startPosition: seated tall with back and head against the pad, feet flat, forearms/hands on the lever pads with elbows at chest height and a soft bend
endPosition: pads squeezed together in front of the chest via shoulder horizontal adduction with a brief peak-contraction pause, then controlled return until a gentle chest stretch with elbows no further back than the torso plane
rom: shoulder horizontal adduction through the available range, controlled return to a mild horizontal-abduction stretch at the torso plane

startPosition: supine, knees bent ~90°, feet flat hip-width on the floor, arms at sides, one hand optionally under the lumbar spine
endPosition: pelvis returned to and held in neutral (lumbar spine lightly touching the floor) for three breaths, after cycling through full posterior and anterior tilt
rom: lumbopelvic tilt through full available anterior-to-posterior pelvic rotation (~10–15° each direction), rocking about the hip sockets

startPosition: standing, trunk leaned forward at the hips ~45–90°, supporting the uninvolved hand on a table, affected arm hanging relaxed and vertical
endPosition: affected arm continuing passive small circular/pendular sway driven by trunk motion, shoulder muscles relaxed throughout
rom: passive glenohumeral excursion tracing a small (~15–30 cm / coffee-cup-sized) circle; motion generated by body sway, not active shoulder ROM

startPosition: supine, knees bent, feet flat, relaxed, one hand on lower ribs/belly
endPosition: end of a slow 4–6 s 360° inhale with belly expanded and pelvic floor passively descended; then passive recoil on gentle exhale — no active contraction
rom: minimal joint excursion; ribcage/diaphragm expansion with passive pelvic floor descent and recoil during each breath cycle

startPosition: supine modified butterfly: soles of feet together, knees dropped apart and supported by pillows, arms relaxed (or deep supported squat / child's pose variants)
endPosition: sustained gravity-assisted end-range hip abduction/external rotation with pelvic floor passively lengthened, held 60–90 s with diaphragmatic breathing
rom: static hold at comfortable end-range hip abduction/ER; minimal ongoing joint excursion — passive lengthening only

startPosition: supine, knees bent, feet flat, pelvic floor and abdomen relaxed
endPosition: maximal pelvic floor contraction held 1–2 s then fully released, repeated rapidly with complete relaxation between reps
rom: isometric pelvic floor contraction — no visible joint movement; rapid on/off internal muscle excursion

startPosition: forward-kneeling (quadruped or kneeling leaning slightly forward), hands on belly, abdomen and pelvic floor relaxed
endPosition: after a quick abdominal sniff and outward belly flop, the pelvic floor sphincters consciously released/dropped like a sigh into the pelvis
rom: minimal joint excursion; abdominal wall expansion with downward pelvic floor release — a relaxation/downtrain drill, not a contraction

startPosition: supine, knees bent, feet flat, pelvic floor relaxed, breathing normally (progresses to sitting then standing)
endPosition: pelvic floor contracted and lifted upward toward the head, held 6–10 s, then fully relaxed for an equal interval
rom: isometric pelvic floor lift held sub-maximally; no external joint motion — internal muscle excursion only

startPosition: standing upright, relaxed, anticipating a cough/sneeze/lift or intentional practice cough
endPosition: brisk pelvic floor pre-contraction timed just before and sustained through the pressure spike, bracing the floor against downward load
rom: isometric anticipatory pelvic floor contraction; minimal joint excursion — timing/coordination skill

startPosition: from quadruped/prone, front leg bent and drawn across the body (hip flexed ~120°, externally rotated ~50°, adducted ~30°), back leg extended straight behind, both pelvic halves squared toward the floor
endPosition: trunk lengthened and lowered over the front shin, back foot reaching away, holding the deep buttock stretch at end range
rom: static end-range hip flexion/external rotation/adduction of the front hip with hip extension of the trailing leg; held stretch with minimal movement

startPosition: seated, forearm supported on a table in neutral, wrist quiet, thumb and finger(s) positioned to grip putty or a pinch gauge
endPosition: thumb pressed against finger(s) in tip, three-jaw-chuck, or lateral/key pinch to the target load, squeeze held ~5 s then released
rom: small thumb-finger closing excursion through the pinch; isometric hold at target force with an isolated, stationary wrist

startPosition: varies by drill: prone with legs straight for hamstring curls, seated with feet flat for heel raises, or tall kneeling for Nordic eccentrics; spine long and neutral
endPosition: posterior chain fully shortened (heel pulled toward the buttock / heels raised / trunk lowered under control) then returned over ~3 s eccentric
rom: full available knee flexion (curls), ankle plantarflexion (heel raises), or controlled hip/knee eccentric lengthening (Nordics)

startPosition: standing, barbell held in the hang position at mid-thigh, hips hinged back, chest over the bar, neutral spine, shoulders slightly ahead of the bar
endPosition: explosive triple extension to fully tall (ankles, knees, hips extended) with the bar caught in the front-rack across the shoulders, elbows high
rom: large dynamic excursion from mid-thigh hang through full hip/knee/ankle extension, then re-flexion to receive the bar in a front-rack partial squat

startPosition: prone on a table, shoulder abducted to 90° with the upper arm supported on the table (towel under the arm), elbow bent 90° hanging off the edge, forearm/dumbbell pointing toward the floor
endPosition: shoulder externally rotated so the forearm/knuckles rotate up toward the ceiling, dumbbell lifted along an arc to horizontal, scapula packed
rom: glenohumeral external rotation from ~0° (forearm vertical, down) through ~90° ER (forearm horizontal) at 90° abduction

startPosition: Prone, forehead resting on hands or floor, both legs straight, pelvis flat on the mat
endPosition: One leg lifted toward the ceiling via glute contraction with a soft-straight knee, no lumbar arching, held 2 s at the top
rom: 0–~15° hip extension from the floor (glute-limited, low back stays neutral)

startPosition: Prone, forehead down, arms hanging off the sides in a Y or T with thumbs pointing up (shoulders externally rotated)
endPosition: Arms lifted toward the ceiling, thumbs up, scapulae retracted and depressed with mid/lower trap engaged and upper trap quiet
rom: Arms lift a few inches off the floor; glenohumeral horizontal abduction with scapular retraction to end range

startPosition: Lying face-down on the floor, hands under the shoulders, hips/buttocks/low back fully relaxed and resting on the mat
endPosition: Upper body pressed up on extended arms with pelvis still resting on the floor, lumbar spine passively extended to pain-free/centralizing range
rom: Progressive lumbar extension, pressing up only as far as symptoms allow, gaining range each rep

startPosition: Prone, forehead on a towel, arms abducted to 90° in a T position with thumbs pointing up
endPosition: Arms lifted toward the ceiling with shoulders externally rotated and scapulae retracted/depressed, elbows drawn toward the back hip pockets
rom: Arms lift a few inches off floor; 90° horizontal abduction with scapular retraction/depression to end range

startPosition: Prone, forehead on a towel, arms elevated overhead in a Y (~135° abduction) with thumbs pointing up
endPosition: Arms lifted toward the ceiling at 11 and 1 o'clock, shoulder blades tucked down and together, lower trap engaged
rom: Arms lift a few inches off floor; scapular upward rotation/depression driving overhead arm elevation to end range

startPosition: Prone, forehead down, ribs relaxed on the floor, arms hanging off the sides, thumbs pointing up
endPosition: Arms lifted a few inches to trace Y (overhead V), then T (straight out), then W (elbows bent toward ribs), each led by the shoulder blades and paused ~2 s
rom: Arms lift only a few inches; sequential scapular retraction/depression and upward rotation through Y, T, and W positions

startPosition: Full vertical hang from an overhead bar, pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder width, arms fully extended, trunk braced
endPosition: Body pulled up with elbows driven down and back until the chin clears the bar, then lowered under control to a full hang
rom: Full elbow flexion and shoulder extension/adduction; complete hang to chin-over-bar and back

startPosition: Seated upright, shoulders relaxed, one hand may rest on the belly, neutral spine
endPosition: Slow nasal inhale with the belly rising, then a quiet pursed-lip exhale (lips as if cooling soup) for twice the inhale duration, belly falling
rom: Isometric posture — minimal joint excursion; movement limited to diaphragmatic belly rise/fall and rib expansion

startPosition: Prone plank/push-up start, hands under shoulders, body straight from crown to heel
endPosition: At the top of the push-up, scapulae fully protracted (upper back rounds, serratus wraps the ribs), chest pushed maximally away from the floor
rom: Full push-up elbow flexion/extension plus additional end-range scapular protraction (the 'plus')

startPosition: SLR: supine with the exercising knee locked straight, opposite knee bent, pelvis level. TKE: standing with a band behind the knee
endPosition: SLR: locked leg lifted to ~45° with quad driving the lift. TKE: knee extended the final 30° pushing the back of the knee into the band
rom: SLR: 0–45° hip flexion with knee locked; TKE: terminal ~30–0° knee extension

startPosition: Quadruped on hands and knees, spine long and neutral, hands under shoulders and knees under hips
endPosition: Hips rocked back toward the heels with the lumbar spine gently rounding, stopping where the low back still feels comfortable
rom: Hips travel back toward heels producing hip flexion with gentle lumbar flexion, to comfortable end range

startPosition: Seated upright, arm at the side, shoulder relaxed in neutral
endPosition: Radial nerve tensioned through sequential shoulder depression, elbow extension, forearm pronation, and wrist flexion, then eased back to slide the nerve
rom: Oscillating neural glide combining shoulder depression, full elbow extension, forearm pronation, and wrist flexion to gentle end-range tension

startPosition: Side-lying (or standing) with a towel trapped under the elbow, elbow flexed 90° at the side, forearm across the abdomen against band/cable resistance
endPosition: Forearm rotated outward away from the body's centerline, wrist pointing toward the far wall, elbow staying pinned to the side
rom: Glenohumeral external rotation from ~internal/neutral to end-range ER with the elbow fixed at the side

startPosition: standing tall, feet hip-width, weight even, arms relaxed at sides, trunk upright with ribs stacked over pelvis
endPosition: split stance with one foot stepped behind, back knee lowered toward the floor and front knee bent near 90°, front shin near-vertical with knee tracking over second toe, trunk still upright
rom: hip and knee flexion 0→~90° in the stepping-back stance; return to full hip/knee extension in standing

startPosition: standing tall holding barbell/dumbbells at front of thighs, knees softly bent ~15–20° and held fixed, spine neutral, load close to legs
endPosition: hips pushed back into a deep hinge, torso inclined forward with neutral spine, bar dragged down to mid-shin/just below the knee at firm hamstring stretch, knee angle unchanged
rom: hip flexion 0→~80–90° of hinge with near-constant knee angle; return to full hip extension standing

startPosition: seated, forearm/hand supported, fingers long and straight and held together with an elastic band looped around them
endPosition: fingers actively spread wide into full abduction against the band, open shape held ~3 seconds before controlled release
rom: MCP abduction from fingers adducted (0°) to full active spread against band resistance

startPosition: seated on floor in a reclined V-sit, trunk leaned back ~45° with long spine and lifted chest, knees bent, heels resting light, hands clasped (or weight) in front of chest
endPosition: trunk rotated so the breastbone points to one side and the hands/weight are brought down beside that hip near the floor, reclined trunk angle and abdominal brace maintained
rom: trunk axial rotation from neutral to comfortable end range each side (hip to hip), reclined trunk angle held constant

startPosition: seated with head still and upright, two near targets held ~2 ft apart and 1–2 ft from the face (horizontal pair first, then vertical)
endPosition: gaze rapidly and accurately refixated onto the opposite target with no drift or overshoot, head kept motionless throughout
rom: rapid horizontal then vertical saccadic eye excursions between targets; head/neck isometric with minimal joint excursion

startPosition: standing tall, long spine, arms at sides with thumbs up, free hand resting on the upper trap
endPosition: arms elevated to about shoulder height in the scapular plane (30–45° anterior to coronal), thumbs up and externally rotated so hands aim toward the 11 and 1 o'clock ceiling corners
rom: shoulder elevation 0→~90° in the scapular plane combined with glenohumeral external rotation

startPosition: standing facing a wall with both forearms and elbows in light contact at shoulder height
endPosition: forearms having traced a full slow clockwise (then counter-clockwise) circle on the wall and returned to start, scapulae gliding around the rib cage throughout
rom: full scapulothoracic circumduction (protraction, elevation, retraction, depression) through the clock-face circle, forearms staying on the wall

startPosition: standing with the arm elevated overhead, long spine and quiet shoulder, free hand on the shoulder blade
endPosition: arm slowly lowered along an arc down to the back hip pocket over ~4 seconds, scapula eccentrically downwardly rotating and gliding smoothly
rom: controlled eccentric arm lowering from overhead to hip with scapular downward rotation through available range

startPosition: seated (or standing) tall with long neck and soft upper traps, arms relaxed, chest lifted
endPosition: shoulder blades squeezed together in retraction (pinching an imaginary pencil), back of shirt drawn toward the chair, held briefly
rom: scapular retraction from neutral to end range; small gross-joint excursion focused at the scapulothoracic joint

startPosition: standing tall with long neck, arms at sides (or abducted to 90°/150° to bias upward rotators)
endPosition: shoulders first elevated toward the earlobes (shrug), then driven down toward the back pockets into depression, pausing at top and bottom
rom: full scapular elevation and depression excursion between end-range shrug and end-range depression

startPosition: seated tall (or standing) with long neck and lifted breastbone, holding a resistance band with arms extended forward
endPosition: band pulled toward the lower ribs with elbows driven back toward the hip pockets, lower shoulder blades retracted and depressed at end range
rom: shoulder extension with elbow flexion and scapular retraction/depression through the rowing pull, then controlled return

startPosition: standing tall with long spine, lifted breastbone, soft jaw, crown of head reaching toward the ceiling
endPosition: sustained three-dimensionally corrected posture — spine elongated, trunk deflected and derotated — while breathing into the concave/collapsed side of the ribcage
rom: active postural correction combining axial elongation, deflection and derotation with facilitated rib breathing; largely isometric with minimal gross joint excursion

startPosition: seated tall at the cable station, knees slightly bent, feet braced on the platform, holding the handle with arms fully extended and trunk upright
endPosition: handle pulled to the lower ribs/upper abdomen with elbows driven straight back and shoulder blades squeezed together, trunk still and upright
