rom: shoulder extension with elbow flexion and scapular retraction through the full pull, then controlled return to full-reach protraction

startPosition: seated upright, knees bent ~90°, balls of both feet on a raised platform with heels hanging free and dropped below platform level, resistance pad/weight across the lower thighs just above the knees
endPosition: heels driven up as high as possible into full plantarflexion, briefly squeezed at the top with knees still bent ~90°
rom: ankle plantarflexion from a dorsiflexed heel-below-platform stretch through to full plantarflexion; knee held static at ~90° to bias the soleus

startPosition: seated tall on the sit-bones, one ankle crossed onto the opposite knee (figure-4) so the crossed hip sits in flexion, abduction, and external rotation, spine upright
endPosition: trunk hinged forward from the hip with a long spine until a stretch is felt in the glute/piriformis of the crossed leg, chest leading
rom: hip flexion via forward trunk hinge to comfortable stretch end range; crossed hip sustained in external rotation/abduction

startPosition: seated on the edge of a chair, feet flat on the floor, knees pointing forward over the toes, pelvis level with both sit-bones equally weighted
endPosition: shin swept outward so the foot moves away from the centerline, producing hip internal rotation, knee still pointing forward and trunk quiet
rom: hip internal rotation from neutral to available end range

startPosition: seated tall against the chair back, thigh fully supported, foot flat, knee bent ~90°
endPosition: knee straightened so the leg is nearly parallel to the floor, quadriceps (especially the VMO above/inside the kneecap) firmly tightened and held ~2 s at the top
rom: knee extension from ~90° flexion to near-0° (full extension)

startPosition: seated tall, hips and pelvis square facing forward, both hands placed on the opposite knee
endPosition: upper body rotated toward one side with the pelvis staying square, eyes following the turn to add cervical rotation
rom: thoracic (with added cervical) axial rotation to comfortable end range, pelvis fixed

startPosition: seated at a table with a mirror positioned in the midline, the unaffected hand in front of the mirror and the affected hand hidden behind it, gaze on the mirror reflection
endPosition: identifying textures, shapes, and touch location (stereognosis/locognosia) with the affected hand while watching the reflected unaffected hand, progressing eyes-open to eyes-closed
rom: isometric/perceptual sensory task — minimal joint excursion; light hand and finger movements only

startPosition: standing tall gripping a band/cable, working elbow tucked at the side and bent 90° with a towel trapped under the elbow, forearm rotated outward pointing away from the body
endPosition: forearm rotated inward across the body toward the belly, wrist pointing at the opposite hip, elbow still pinned to the side and towel still trapped
rom: glenohumeral internal rotation from an externally rotated start through neutral to across the midline

startPosition: sidelying on the down forearm with elbow directly under the shoulder, legs stacked and extended, hips resting toward the floor
endPosition: hips driven upward so the body forms one straight line from head to feet, down hip pushed toward the ceiling, position held
rom: isometric lateral-bridge hold — minimal joint excursion once hips are lifted

startPosition: side-lying on the uninvolved side with a towel roll under the working elbow, elbow bent 90° and pinned to the side, forearm rotated down across the abdomen holding a light dumbbell
endPosition: shoulder externally rotated so the dumbbell rotates up toward the ceiling and the knuckles aim at the far wall, towel still trapped under the elbow
rom: glenohumeral external rotation from internal/neutral up to end range, elbow fixed at 90°

startPosition: sidelying on the down forearm with elbow under the shoulder, feet/legs stacked, ribs over hips, hips toward the floor
endPosition: hips lifted so the body is one straight line from head to heel, top hip reaching toward the ceiling, position held
rom: isometric side-plank hold — minimal joint excursion once elevated

startPosition: established side-plank position on the down forearm, hips lifted into a straight head-to-heel line, top leg stacked on the bottom leg
endPosition: top leg lifted into hip abduction with the heel reaching toward the ceiling while the side-plank line is maintained, then lowered under control
rom: hip abduction ~0–30° of the top leg superimposed on a sustained isometric side-plank hold

startPosition: standing on one leg with a soft (unlocked) standing knee, level pelvis and quiet hip, opposite foot lifted off the floor, trunk upright
endPosition: free-leg toe reaching out to lightly tap a target line in a given direction (progressing through 8 star directions) at maximum reach while single-leg stance is maintained, standing knee still tracking over the toes
rom: multidirectional free-leg reach excursions (anterior/posterolateral/posteromedial through 8 directions); standing-limb ankle and hip make small corrective excursions

startPosition: standing on one leg with the rear foot elevated on a bench behind, neutral spine, level pelvis, trunk upright
endPosition: hips pushed back and trunk hinged forward over the standing knee until the trunk and rear leg form one straight beam near parallel to the floor, then driven back to standing by the glute and hamstring
rom: hip flexion via hinge from upright to ~near-horizontal trunk and return to full hip extension; standing knee stays softly bent

startPosition: standing on one leg behind a start line, hands on hips or free to swing, knee softly bent ready to load
endPosition: landed on the same single leg at the target/distance mark, hip and knee flexed to absorb, holding a controlled 2-second stuck landing
rom: explosive triple-extension of hip, knee, and ankle for propulsion then eccentric flexion (~30-60° knee, hip and ankle) to absorb landing; horizontal displacement per test (single, triple, crossover, or 6-m timed)

startPosition: seated tall at the front edge of a wall-braced chair, working foot flat and slightly behind the knee, opposite leg lifted off the floor, trunk upright
endPosition: fully upright standing on the single stance leg with hip and knee extended, non-stance leg still elevated
rom: stance-limb concentric extension from ~90° hip and knee flexion to full extension, then controlled eccentric return; forward trunk hinge (nose over toes) to load and unload

startPosition: standing on one leg, tall trunk, level pelvis, free leg extended forward, arms available for counterbalance
endPosition: deep single-leg squat with the stance knee and hip fully flexed and the free-leg heel reaching forward toward the wall, then driven back to full standing
rom: stance-leg squat through full available depth — hip and knee flexion approaching or exceeding 90-120°, ankle dorsiflexion to end range — then concentric return to full extension

startPosition: standing on one leg on an unstable surface (foam pad, BOSU, or Airex), soft knee, level hip, gaze fixed at eye level, arms ready to assist balance
endPosition: sustained single-leg balance on the unstable surface maintained through continuous ankle micro-corrections (progressed with eyes closed, perturbations, or dual-task)
rom: isometric single-leg hold — minimal joint excursion; small continuous ankle/hip sway corrections about a centered stance

startPosition: seated performing a routine task (typing, reading, drinking) with attention directed to one chosen postural element (foot contact, ribcage stack, or shoulder position)
endPosition: same seated task posture, first held ~30 s of non-judgmental noticing, then ~30 s of gentle deliberate adjustment of that single element
rom: static seated posture — minimal joint excursion; attention-based sensory drill with only small deliberate postural corrections

startPosition: seated on a chair, feet flat hip-width, arms crossed or free (not pushing), long spine, chest ready to lead
endPosition: fully upright standing with hips and knees extended, weight even through both heels
rom: bilateral concentric hip and knee extension from ~90° flexion to full standing with an anterior trunk lean over the toes, then controlled eccentric descent

startPosition: supine hook-lying, knees bent ~90°, feet flat on the floor, arms crossed over chest or hands lightly behind the ears, chin gently tucked
endPosition: torso upright and off the floor, spine segmentally flexed and hips flexed to bring the chest toward the thighs
rom: full trunk flexion — segmental spinal curl through the thoracolumbar spine followed by hip flexion to upright, then controlled segmental eccentric lowering to supine

startPosition: seated upright, long neck, gaze level on the horizon, an elastic band anchored to the ipsilateral side providing an oblique (lateral-flexion + extension) resistance to the head
endPosition: craniocervical flexion nod held with the base of the skull glided back, resisting the band's diagonal pull without rotating the head, SCM staying soft
rom: small-amplitude craniocervical (upper cervical) flexion nod held isometrically against oblique band load — minimal gross cervical excursion, no head turn

startPosition: supine on a flat bench, feet planted, holding an EZ-bar or dumbbells with arms extended over the chest, shoulders flexed ~90°, elbows pointed at the ceiling shoulder-width apart
endPosition: elbows fully flexed with the load lowered under control toward the forehead/just behind the crown, upper arms unmoved, then elbows re-extended to the start over the chest
rom: isolated elbow flexion/extension through roughly 0-120°, shoulders held static at ~90° flexion with no elbow flare or drift

startPosition: sidelying on the target shoulder, that arm forward-flexed to 90° with the elbow bent 90°, scapula packed and the bottom shoulder planted on the surface
endPosition: forearm passively rotated toward the floor into internal rotation, held at the point where a stretch is felt in the back of the joint (short of a sharp edge)
rom: passive glenohumeral internal rotation at 90° abduction/flexion to comfortable posterior-capsule end range; scapula and trunk held still

startPosition: seated upright, head held completely still, gaze directed forward ready to begin tracing a circle
endPosition: gaze having traced a large smooth continuous circle in one direction, then reversed to trace it in the opposite direction, head unmoved throughout
rom: full oculomotor excursion tracing large slow circles (clockwise then counter-clockwise); head isometrically still with no cervical movement

startPosition: seated upright, head still, both eyes fixed on a near target held 1-2 ft from the face
endPosition: eyes having smoothly followed the target through its full slow excursion (~160° horizontal or ~120° vertical) without jerks or head turn
rom: oculomotor smooth pursuit through ~160° horizontal and ~120° vertical gaze excursion; head held stationary with no cervical motion

startPosition: standing tall with a belt/strap around the knees anchored to a fixed point behind, feet flat, trunk upright, shins vertical
endPosition: seated back into the strap with the trunk staying tall and shins vertical at roughly 60° knee flexion, then driven back to full standing
rom: closed-chain knee flexion/extension limited to the low-patellofemoral-force 0-60° range with posterior lean; hips and knees flex against the strap and re-extend to standing

startPosition: Standing in a staggered stance, front foot flat on the floor and rear foot behind on the ground or elevated toe-down on a bench, tall trunk, hips square
endPosition: Bottom of the descent with the rear knee lowered toward the floor, front shin near-vertical, front knee tracking over the second toe and hip/knee deeply flexed
rom: ~0–90°+ front-knee and hip flexion under load, returning to full extension

startPosition: Standing tall, feet about hip- to shoulder-width, long braced spine, arms free or hands on thighs
endPosition: Bottom of squat with hips sent back and down, knees flexed and tracking over the second toe, trunk long and braced
rom: ~0–90°+ combined hip and knee flexion, returning to full standing extension

startPosition: Standing on a stair with balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off, light hand on the rail, ankles neutral
endPosition: Fully plantarflexed at the top, weight pressed up onto the toes with a brief pause, then heels dropped below step level at the bottom
rom: Full ankle range from heels below step level (dorsiflexion stretch) to maximal plantarflexion at the top

startPosition: Standing on a stair with balls of both feet on the step edge, heels hanging free, knees straight, light hand on the handrail
endPosition: Risen as high as possible onto the toes in full plantarflexion with a brief top pause, then heels lowered below the step edge
rom: Full ankle plantarflexion at top to heels-below-edge dorsiflexion stretch at bottom; knees kept straight to bias gastrocnemius

startPosition: Standing at the base of a staircase with one whole foot placed flat (heel supported) on the first step, other foot on the floor
endPosition: Standing fully upright on the step with the working leg extended, having driven up through that heel, then lowered under control to lightly tap the bottom foot
rom: Working leg moves through ~90° knee/hip flexion up to full extension per rep

startPosition: Standing on a step with balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off, holding the railing lightly, heel/hip/shoulder stacked
endPosition: Sustained stretch with heels sunk below step level under body weight, knee straight for gastrocnemius then softly bent to target soleus
rom: Ankle held in end-range dorsiflexion; static hold with minimal joint excursion aside from the straight- to bent-knee shift

startPosition: Standing, one ankle crossed on top of the opposite thigh just above the knee, hand on a counter or doorframe for balance
endPosition: Sitting back into a partial squat on the standing leg with the lifted shin parallel to the floor, feeling a glute/piriformis stretch
rom: Held stretch position; standing-leg hip/knee flex to a shallow squat that sets depth/intensity, otherwise minimal excursion

startPosition: Standing with one heel on a low step or chair, that leg straight, trunk upright with a flat back
endPosition: Hip-hinged forward with chest leading and a flat back until a hamstring stretch is felt, stopping before the spine rounds
rom: Hip flexion via forward hinge to the point of hamstring tension (spine held neutral); static end-range hold

startPosition: Standing tall beside a countertop, one hand resting on it for balance, moving leg straight with kneecap and toes pointing forward
endPosition: Moving leg lifted directly out to the side about 30–45° with trunk kept upright and level, then lowered under control
rom: ~0–45° hip abduction, stopping before pelvic hike; foot kept neutral, no rotation

startPosition: Standing tall, one hand on a wall for balance, one knee lifted to hip height
endPosition: Knee returned through a large circular path, tracing internal-rotation direction first then external, hip moving freely through range
rom: Full circumduction of the hip (flexion, abduction, rotation, extension components) with the trunk held stable

startPosition: Standing with one leg crossed behind the other, same-side arm reaching overhead, trunk tall
endPosition: Lengthened then leaned away to the side with the back-leg hip pushed outward, stretching the lateral chain from hip to underarm
rom: Lateral trunk flexion to comfortable end range plus hip adduction on the back leg; static hold

startPosition: Standing with hands on the low back, fingers pointing down, spine neutral
endPosition: Leaned backward as far as comfortable into lumbar extension, hands supporting as a hinge, held 3 seconds at end-range then returned to neutral
rom: Lumbar extension to pain-free end range, 3-second hold, back to neutral

startPosition: Standing tall in place, arms at sides ready to swing, weight evenly balanced
endPosition: One knee lifted toward hip height with matching arm swing, then alternated to the other side at a steady cadence, landing softly through the ball of the foot
rom: Alternating hip flexion to ~hip-height knee lift with reciprocal arm swing, rhythmic and continuous

startPosition: standing tall on one leg, pelvis neutral, other knee bent with that hand grasping the ankle behind the body
endPosition: heel drawn toward the glute with knees side-by-side and pelvis tucked under, feeling stretch along the front thigh and hip flexor
rom: knee flexion to comfortable end range (~120–135°) with slight hip extension; sustained static stretch hold

startPosition: standing facing, beside, or diagonal to a box/step with one foot planted flat on top and trunk tall, pelvis level
endPosition: fully upright standing on top of the box, working hip and knee extended, trailing toe lightly kissing the box or floor
rom: working knee moves from ~90° flexion to full extension with concurrent hip extension through the full step height (4–30 cm)

startPosition: supine, one knee locked straight, opposite leg extended and pinned to the floor, trunk quiet and pelvis level
endPosition: straight leg lifted with heel toward the ceiling to roughly the height of the opposite bent knee, knee still locked
rom: 0–~45° hip flexion with the knee held in full extension

startPosition: supine, head neutral on the surface, pressure biofeedback cuff behind the neck inflated to 20 mmHg (optional tennis balls under the occiput)
endPosition: gentle craniocervical flexion (chin nod) lengthening the base of the skull, raising the gauge toward 30 mmHg while superficial neck muscles stay quiet
rom: small upper-cervical (C0–C2) nodding through incremental 20→30 mmHg stages; minimal gross neck excursion

startPosition: prone, arms extended overhead, legs straight, neck neutral with gaze at the floor
endPosition: arms, chest, and legs lifted a few centimeters off the floor via spinal and hip extension, held briefly, neck in line with spine
rom: low-amplitude thoracolumbar and hip extension (a few centimeters of lift), brief isometric hold at end range

startPosition: supine with hips and knees flexed to 90°, calves resting on a chair/ottoman/wall, hands on the belly, low back in light contact with the floor
endPosition: sustained 90-90 posture with belly rising before chest during slow diaphragmatic inhalation (~6 breaths/min)
rom: isometric hold — minimal joint excursion; movement is rib/abdominal expansion during breathing

startPosition: supine, head resting on the surface, throat soft, preparing a gentle chin tuck
endPosition: craniocervical flexion maintained while the head floats a card's thickness off the floor, held ~10 s without sternocleidomastoid dominance
rom: small upper-cervical flexion plus minimal head liftoff; sustained endurance hold (~29–39 s norms)

startPosition: supine with a strap looped around the ball of one foot, opposite leg straight or bent on the floor
endPosition: strapped leg raised straight toward the ceiling with a soft knee, gently pulled by the strap until a hamstring stretch is felt
rom: hip flexion toward ~80–90° with the knee near full extension; sustained static stretch

startPosition: supine holding a light dumbbell, arm pointed toward the ceiling with the elbow locked and shoulder blade resting on the table
endPosition: arm punched upward as the scapula protracts 2–3 inches off the table, elbow still locked
rom: scapular protraction of ~2–3 inches (isolated scapulothoracic motion) with the elbow held in extension

startPosition: standing with one foot placed directly in front of the other heel-to-toe along an imaginary line, within arm's reach of a wall or counter
endPosition: steady narrowed heel-to-toe stance maintained with gaze fixed at eye level, correcting with small ankle sways
rom: isometric stance hold — minimal joint excursion aside from small corrective ankle sway

startPosition: seated with the wrist quiet and neutral, fingers extended straight
endPosition: cycled through five finger shapes in order (straight → hook fist → full fist → tabletop → straight fist), holding each ~3 s
rom: full active finger flexion/extension through five differential-glide positions with the wrist held still

startPosition: seated with the wrist quiet and neutral, fingers extended straight
endPosition: cycled through four finger shapes in order (straight → hook fist → straight fist → tabletop), holding each ~3 s
rom: full active finger flexion/extension through four differential-glide positions with the wrist held still

startPosition: seated with a tennis ball placed under the bare foot at the heel
endPosition: ball rolled slowly along the arch to the forefoot with moderate pressure, pausing 5–10 s on tender spots
rom: foot travels heel-to-forefoot over the ball; self-mobilization with minimal active joint motion

startPosition: standing, resistance band looped behind the knee, knee softly flexed ~30°, feet hip-width, hands free or lightly on quad
endPosition: knee fully locked into extension against band tension, kneecap facing straight ahead, VMO engaged at lockout
rom: final ~30° of knee extension (30° flexion to 0° full extension) against band resistance

startPosition: sidelying, hips and knees stacked and bent ~90°, both arms extended together in front at shoulder height, pelvis anchored
endPosition: top arm rotated open toward the wall behind, chest and gaze following the hand across the ceiling into thoracic extension/rotation while hips stay fixed
rom: thoracic rotation to comfortable end range (~open-book to ~90° trunk rotation), lumbar/pelvis held still

startPosition: standing at arm's length from a wall, hands on the wall at shoulder height, spine long, feet hip-width
endPosition: hips hinged back with hands sliding down the wall and chest sinking between the arms into upper thoracic extension
rom: upper thoracic extension through the hip hinge to comfortable end range of shoulder flexion/chest drop

startPosition: supine on a foam roller placed horizontally at mid-thoracic level, knees bent, feet flat, arms crossed over chest or overhead, head supported
endPosition: chest draped open in segmental thoracic extension over the roller, ribs kept from flaring, then roller repositioned one segment higher
rom: segmental thoracic extension over the roller, progressing one vertebral segment at a time up the mid/upper thoracic spine

startPosition: quadruped, hips stacked over knees and shoulders over hands, spine neutral, lumbar locked
endPosition: one hand threaded under the body across midline then opened up toward the ceiling with eyes following, isolating rotation to the thoracic spine
rom: thoracic rotation from fully threaded (arm across midline) to fully open toward the ceiling, ~90°+ arc, lumbar held still

startPosition: seated, forearm supported with wrist neutral and quiet, thumb abducted ready to pinch a key edge between thumb pad and radial side of the index finger
endPosition: thumb adducted firmly into the lateral (key) pinch, adductor pollicis squeezing the key for a sustained 5-second hold
rom: thumb adduction from abducted start to full lateral pinch, then 5-second isometric hold at end range

startPosition: seated, forearm supported, wrist neutral and quiet, proximal thumb joint stabilized by the free hand, thumb IP and MCP in starting neutral
endPosition: isolated thumb IP flexion/extension and MCP flexion/extension performed against light band resistance while the proximal joint is held fixed
rom: full available thumb IP and MCP flexion-extension excursion, each joint moved independently

startPosition: seated, forearm supported, hand open with fingers extended and thumb in neutral abduction
endPosition: thumb pad rotated and touched firmly to each fingertip in sequence forming a clean O, then reversed back through the sequence
rom: full thumb opposition arc (rotation plus flexion) from open hand to pad-to-pad contact with each of the four fingers

startPosition: seated, forearm supported, hand open with fingers extended and thumb neutral
endPosition: thumb pad rotated to face and meet each finger pad in turn (true opposition) forming a clean O, sequencing index to pinky then reversing
rom: full opposition excursion emphasizing thumb pad rotation, pad-to-pad with each finger through the sequence and back

startPosition: seated, forearm supported, wrist neutral and quiet, therapy putty held in the hand with thumb positioned to press or squeeze
endPosition: thumb pad pressed into the putty against each fingertip and putty squeezed into a ball, working opposition, abduction, adduction, and pinch under resistance
rom: full thumb opposition/abduction/adduction and pinch excursion against putty resistance across the program tasks

startPosition: seated (or standing) with foot flat on the floor, all toes relaxed on the ground
endPosition: the four lesser toes pressed down while the big toe lifts, then reversed so the big toe presses down while the four lesser toes lift, with a finger holding down the toes that should stay put
rom: selective toe MTP flexion/extension — big toe extension with lesser-toe flexion and vice versa, small isolated excursion

startPosition: supine, knees bent, heels on a folded towel on a smooth floor, arms at sides
endPosition: hips lifted into a bridge (knees-hips-shoulders straight line) with heels slid out until legs are nearly straight, then dragged back in by curling the knees, hips held level throughout
rom: eccentric-to-concentric knee flexion/extension from ~90° bent to near-full extension and back, hips maintained in bridge

startPosition: standing, towel looped around both handles of a closed latched door, an end held in each hand, feet walked in so the body leans back with arms straight
endPosition: chest pulled toward the door with elbows driven back and down and shoulder blades squeezed together at end range, straight line from heels to head
rom: horizontal pull through full elbow flexion and scapular retraction from arms straight to chest near the door

startPosition: seated or standing tall with neutral spine, chin gently tucked and ribs down, both hands gripping a rolled bath towel shoulder-width apart, arms extended in front at chest height with elbows slightly bent
endPosition: shoulder blades squeezed down and back toward the spine while pulling outward on the taut towel, elbows still slightly bent, held isometrically 3-5 seconds
rom: scapular retraction and depression to end range; isometric hold with minimal joint excursion at the elbows

startPosition: seated tall, upper arm vertical alongside the head, elbow fully flexed with the dumbbell/band lowered behind the head, shoulder quiet
endPosition: elbow fully extended driving the dumbbell straight toward the ceiling, upper arm still vertical
rom: elbow flexion to extension, roughly 0-130° through full available elbow range; shoulder held stationary

startPosition: standing facing a cable column with slight forward hip hinge, chest tall, elbows pinned to the ribs, forearms raised to about 90° of elbow flexion gripping the bar/rope at upper-chest height
endPosition: elbows fully extended pressing the handle down toward the thighs at lockout, elbows still pinned to the sides
rom: elbow extension from ~90° flexion to full lockout (~90-0°); elbows fixed at the sides

startPosition: supine, weight pressed straight up over the shoulder with a packed shoulder and long spine, same-side knee bent and foot flat, eyes fixed on the bell
endPosition: fully standing tall with the weight locked out overhead, having transitioned through roll-to-elbow, hand, half-kneel, and stand stations
rom: full-body multi-joint transition from supine to standing; hip, knee, trunk, and shoulder move through large ranges while the loaded shoulder stays fixed overhead

startPosition: standing, holding a FlexBar with the affected wrist maximally extended and the bar twisted taut, forearm long and supported
endPosition: wrist controlled slowly from extension into flexion as the FlexBar untwists over about three seconds, extensors lengthening under load
rom: eccentric wrist extension-to-flexion arc, ~full available wrist extension down to neutral/flexion; slow controlled untwist

startPosition: seated upright with a neutral spine, mouth closed, relaxed shoulders, ready to breathe through the nose with gentle glottal narrowing
endPosition: sustained slow diaphragmatic breathing cycle with soft audible glottal constriction on both inhale and exhale, thorax gently expanding and recoiling
rom: rib-cage and diaphragm excursion of quiet breathing; isometric posture with minimal joint movement

startPosition: seated upright, working arm at the side, ready to sequence shoulder abduction, elbow flexion, wrist extension, and cervical lateral flexion
endPosition: shoulder abducted with elbow flexed and wrist extended bringing the open hand up toward the ear, head tipped away from the working arm to load the nerve, then released
rom: combined shoulder abduction, elbow flexion, wrist extension, and cervical lateral flexion producing sliding neural excursion at the cubital tunnel; light non-tensioning glide

startPosition: seated or standing with a long, tall neutral spine, target-side shoulder gently depressed, same-side fingertips reaching toward the floor
endPosition: cervical spine laterally flexed away from the target side, opposite ear drawn toward the opposite shoulder while the ipsilateral shoulder stays depressed, held at a comfortable stretch
rom: cervical lateral flexion to comfortable end range with scapular depression; sustained static hold

startPosition: standing tall with a long spine, arms abducted 30° in the coronal plane holding dumbbells floating out from the sides, shoulders relaxed down
endPosition: shoulders elevated toward the earlobes (shrug) while the arms maintain the 30° abducted position, biasing the upward rotators at the top
rom: scapular elevation/upward rotation through full shrug range; arms held statically at 30° abduction

startPosition: seated (or the specific provoking posture) ready to perform 2-3 self-selected symptom-provoking positions or movements identified from a Motion Sensitivity Quotient or symptom diary
endPosition: the provoking position or movement completed and held/repeated to a tolerable symptom level, then rested until symptoms fully settle within 60 seconds
rom: repeated controlled head/body excursions specific to each provoking movement; graded to tolerable symptom provocation

startPosition: seated, eyes fixated on a stationary target held at arm's length, head in neutral
endPosition: target kept in clear focus while the head is rotated side-to-side and then up-and-down at the fastest pace that preserves target clarity
rom: active cervical rotation and flexion/extension oscillations to comfortable amplitude while gaze stays fixed; target stationary

startPosition: seated, holding a target in front at arm's length with eyes fixated on it, head neutral
endPosition: head and target moved simultaneously in opposite directions at matched speed while the target stays sharply in focus
rom: cervical rotation oscillations paired with equal opposite target motion, doubling angular VOR demand; amplitude limited to where the target stays clear

startPosition: standing tall, feet together, ready to step forward into a lunge
endPosition: in the bottom of a forward lunge (front knee tracking over the toes, back knee near the floor) with the trunk rotated toward the front leg, then returned before stepping through to the next lunge
rom: large hip and knee flexion of the lunge combined with thoracic rotation toward the lead leg; alternating stepping gait

startPosition: Standing with back, hips, and head lightly touching a wall; ribs gently drawn down so low back is only lightly arched; arms in a goalpost/'W' position with backs of hands and forearms pressed flat to the wall, elbows at shoulder height
endPosition: Arms slid overhead into a 'Y'/near-full-elevation position with wrists, forearms, and all contact points still on the wall, then returned to the start goalpost
rom: Shoulder elevation from ~90° (goalpost) toward ~160-170° overhead and back, limited to where wrists stay on the wall without shrugging or low-back lifting; roughly 3 s up, 3 s down

startPosition: Standing with an exercise ball pinned between the low back and a wall, feet about a foot ahead of the hips and shoulder-width apart, knees nearly straight
endPosition: Squatted down with knees bent toward ~90°, shins vertical, knees stacked over mid-foot, ball rolled up along the spine; then pressed back up through the heels or held
rom: Knee/hip flexion from near-full extension to ~90° knee flexion (or shallower if painful), controlled closed-chain descent and ascent

startPosition: Supine in a doorway, buttock close to the door frame, the working leg raised straight and resting against the wall/door frame, the opposite leg extended flat through the doorway, low back and pelvis relaxed on the floor
endPosition: Working leg held with knee straight and hip flexed to a firm but tolerable hamstring stretch, ankle dorsiflexed (toes pulled toward face) for a calf/neural component; sustained statically
rom: Hip flexion held near the point of first firm hamstring tension (commonly ~60-90° depending on flexibility) with the knee kept in full extension; static hold, minimal joint excursion once positioned

startPosition: Standing arm's length from a wall, body straight as a plank from crown to heel, hands flat on the wall at about chest height and shoulder-width apart, elbows extended
endPosition: Chest brought toward the wall as elbows bend, then pressed back to full elbow extension with scapulae protracted (upper back driven toward the ceiling/away from the wall)
rom: Elbow flexion/extension through available range with concurrent scapular protraction and shoulder flexion; body held rigid, no hip or trunk motion

startPosition: Back flat against a wall, feet hip-width apart and about 1.5-2 foot-lengths from the wall, then slid down until hips and knees are bent to roughly 90° with shins vertical and knees stacked over ankles
endPosition: Same ~90° hip and knee flexion squat position held isometrically against the wall while breathing steadily, then slid back up to standing to finish
rom: Isometric hold at ~90° hip and knee flexion — minimal joint excursion during the hold; entry/exit slide through the descent/ascent range

startPosition: Standing with back flat against a wall, feet shoulder-width apart and one to two foot-lengths out from the wall, knees near extension
endPosition: Slid down until thighs approach parallel to the floor (~90° hip and knee flexion, or higher if too intense), whole back and low ribs pressed into the wall, weight even through both heels; position held statically
rom: Isometric hold at up to ~90° hip and knee flexion (graded shallower as needed) — minimal joint excursion during the sustained hold

startPosition: Standing facing a wall with forearms and hands on the wall, spine long, breastbone lifted, lower ribs anchored against the wall, elbows below shoulder height
endPosition: Forearms slid upward along the wall toward the ceiling while maintaining scapular posterior tilt and upward rotation, wrists pressing steadily into the wall, lower ribs still anchored
rom: Shoulder elevation with scapular upward rotation from ~90° toward overhead, limited to the range where lower-trap control and rib anchoring are maintained; controlled slide up and down

startPosition: Standing facing or perpendicular to a wall, spine tall and lifted, hand of the involved arm placed on the wall at a comfortable low starting height, fingers ready to climb
endPosition: Fingers walked progressively up the wall to a new high point (marked with tape) that increases shoulder flexion or abduction into mild discomfort but not sharp pain, then walked back down
rom: Progressive shoulder flexion or abduction increasing session-to-session toward end range, worked only into mild discomfort; graded active-assisted elevation

startPosition: Standing tall, a filled water bottle in each hand resting at the sides, elbows soft (slightly bent), shoulders down and neck relaxed
endPosition: Both arms raised out to the sides in the frontal plane to about shoulder height with elbows leading and bottles just below hand height, then lowered slowly under control
rom: Shoulder abduction from ~0° to ~90° (stopping at shoulder height, no higher), lowering over 2-3 s

startPosition: Standing, then step into a long forward lunge with both hands on the floor framing the lead foot and the trailing knee lowered toward the ground to load the trailing-side hip flexors
endPosition: From the loaded lunge, lead-side elbow sinks toward the instep, then the outside hand stays planted while the trunk rotates and the lead-side arm reaches to the ceiling (eyes following the hand); finally hips shift back and the front knee straightens to lengthen the hamstring, then return to lunge and step to stand
rom: Chained multi-joint excursion: deep hip/knee lunge flexion, trailing-hip extension, thoracic rotation to end range on the reach, and hip flexion with knee extension for the hamstring lengthening; move slowly, pause 1-2 s at the top of the rotation, alternate sides

startPosition: Seated with the forearm resting on the thigh, wrist and hand hanging just past the knee, holding a light dumbbell; performed both palm-up (flexion) and palm-down (extension)
endPosition: Wrist curled up toward the ceiling at the top of each rep, then lowered past horizontal into the stretched end range while the forearm stays glued to the thigh
rom: Wrist flexion-to-extension through full available range in each direction, forearm stationary; ~3 s eccentric lowering

startPosition: Standing with the elbow fully extended and the arm reached forward, wrist neutral, the opposite hand grasping the back of the target hand
endPosition: Free hand gently flexes the target wrist (pressing the back of the hand toward the floor) until a firm but non-sharp stretch is felt over the forearm extensors, then held
rom: Wrist flexion to comfortable end range with elbow held in full extension; static hold, minimal joint excursion once positioned

startPosition: Standing with the elbow fully extended and the arm reached forward, palm facing away, the opposite hand grasping the palm and fingers of the target hand
endPosition: Free hand gently extends the target wrist and fingers (pressing palm and fingers toward the floor) until a firm but non-sharp stretch is felt over the forearm flexors, then held
rom: Wrist and finger extension to comfortable end range with elbow held in full extension; static hold, minimal joint excursion once positioned

startPosition: seated, forearm resting along the thigh in thumb-up (neutral) rotation, wrist and hand hanging just off the knee/edge holding a light dumbbell, wrist in neutral alignment
endPosition: hand swept up into radial deviation (thumb side toward the ceiling), then reversed down into ulnar deviation (pinky side toward the floor), forearm unmoving
rom: radial deviation ~0–20° and ulnar deviation ~0–30° from neutral; motion isolated to the wrist with the forearm and elbow held still

startPosition: seated, forearm supported flat on a table with the hand/wrist off the edge, holding a dumbbell or band handle, wrist in neutral
endPosition: wrist/forearm loaded to end range in each of six directions in turn — full flexion, full extension, radial deviation, ulnar deviation, pronation, and supination — pausing ~2 s at each end range before reversing
rom: through full available range in all six planes: flexion/extension ~0–70° each, radial ~0–20°/ulnar ~0–30° deviation, and forearm pronation/supination ~0–80° each; shoulder and elbow stay quiet

startPosition: seated with the forearm supported and the wrist in neutral (0–20° extension); a rigid splint holds the wrist neutral for nighttime wear, and for daytime glides the hand begins with fingers and wrist in a straight, neutral resting position
endPosition: for splinting, the wrist is maintained static in neutral; for the daytime glides, the fingers and wrist cycle through successive nerve- and tendon-glide end positions (straight-hand/wrist extension, hook fist, full fist, tabletop, and median-nerve-loaded wrist-and-finger extension)
rom: splint component is an isometric hold with minimal joint excursion (wrist fixed at 0–20° extension); the tendon- and nerve-gliding component moves the fingers and wrist through their full available range every 2–3 hours
